name: Module 1: Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and Benefits
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about various programming paradigms, principles of OOP and its benefits in enhancing software development practices.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Describe various programming paradigms
  2. Explain the principles of Object-Oriented Programming
  3. Evaluate the benefits of OOP
  4. Apply OOP principles in Software Development

  6. Review concept assignment : Quiz 2

modules.name: Take Home Message Self Reflection We consider a si...
modules.description:
Take Home Message

Self Reflection

We consider a simple program that calculates the area of a rectangle. This program can be implemented in both a procedural programming language like C and an object-oriented programming language like Java. 

Show the way the program is structured and organized and bring out the programming paradigms.

modules.name: Comparing C and Java
modules.description: We consider a simple program that calculates the area of a rectangle. This program can be implemented in both a procedural programming language like C and an object-oriented programming language like Java. Show the way the program is structured and organized and bring out the programming paradigms.

name: Module 2: Classes and Objects
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about the concept of classes, create classes to represent objects in software development and apply them appropriately.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Implement Classes in Object-Oriented Programming
  2. Utilize Constructors and Destructors in Class Definitions
  3. Develop and Test Object-Oriented Programs

name: Module 3: Methods and Encapsulation
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about how to define methods within classes, apply Modifiers to control the visibility and accessibility of class members, and Use data hiding techniques to secure the internal state of objects.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Define and Implement Methods in Classes
  2. Utilize Access Modifiers for Member Visibility
  3. Implement Encapsulation to Protect Data
  4. Apply Data Hiding Techniques in Class Design

name: Module 4: Polymorphism and Overloading
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about the concept of polymorphism and demonstrate its application, implement operator overloading and distinguish between overloading as a member function and as a non-member function.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Explain and Apply Polymorphism in OOP
  2. Implement Operator Overloading in Classes
  3. Apply Overloading Principles
  4. Differentiate and Implement Member and Non-Member Function Overloading

name: Module 5: Inheritance
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about the fundamental concept of inheritance in OOP, different types of inheritance, use of inheritance to enhance code reuse and class hierarchies.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Apply Inheritance in Object-Oriented Programming
  2. Identify and Differentiate Between Types of Inheritance
  3. Utilize Inheritance to Enhance Code Reuse
  4. Design and Implement Effective Class Hierarchies

name: Module 6: Object Relationships
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about the concept of association, aggregation, composition, dependency and their application to implement robust and maintainable class structures.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Apply the Concept of Association in Object-Oriented Programming
  2. Differentiate Between Aggregation and Composition
  3. Analyze and Implement Dependency Relationships
  4. Design Class Structures Using Association, Aggregation, Composition, and Dependency

name: Module 7: Abstract Classes and Interfaces
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about the concept of abstract classes and methods, purpose of interfaces in OOP and application of the concepts of abstract classes and interfaces to real-world applications.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Apply Abstract Classes and Methods
  2. Define and Implement Interfaces
  3. Differentiate Between Abstract Classes and Interfaces
  4. Apply the Concepts abstract classes and interfaces to Real-World Applications

Let's Dive In!

Learning Materials

We begin our journey with a comprehensive collection of Learning Materials designed to build your foundational knowledge. Here you'll find videos, readings, and resources meticulously curated to prepare you for practical applications and deeper understanding.

modules.name: Abstract Classes and Methods in Java Explained in 7 Minutes
modules.description: Watch this video on abstract classes and interfaces and customize the example to a program of your choice but different from what is given in the video.

contents.filename: Abstract Classes and Methods in Java Explained in 7 Minutes

modules.name: Abstract Classes vs Interfaces (Java)
modules.description: Below is a video  to explain the abstract classes and interfaces in Java. Listen and write a one page report on the same different programming paradigms

modules.name: Milestone 1: Analyze Context and Situation
modules.description:
Lectures, readings, and other instructional activities

Click on the Moodle "Book" below and go through each section. Click on the green arrows (previous and Next) to move from one section to another. You will need to go through the Moodle Book first in order to successfully complete the Discussion and Journal Activities.

Task 2: Experience

We would like to know whether you have used an LMS before so as to build on your experience or the lack of it, to support your learning map. If you have used an LMS previously; please click below to identify the LMS you are familiar with.

modules.name: Engage and Explore! Activities and Engagement Now ...
modules.description:
Engage and Explore!

Activities and Engagement

Now that you've absorbed the basics, it's time to put your knowledge into action. In this section, you'll participate in interactive activities and discussions that challenge you to think critically and apply what you've learned in dynamic ways.

modules.name: Understanding Data Types and the Core Concepts of Object-Oriented Programming
modules.description: Discuss the differences between qualitative and quantitative data, providing an example of each. Additionally, explain the key principles of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and how it contrasts with other programming paradigms.

modules.description:
Test Your Knowledge!

Assessment

Ready to show what you've learned? This section includes quizzes and assignments to assess your understanding of the course material. Each activity is designed to reinforce your learning and provide feedback on your progress.

modules.name: Test Your Knowledge
modules.description:
Challenge yourself with our quizzes designed to reinforce your learning and test your understanding of the material.

Time required: x minutes

modules.name: Practical application of abstract classes and interfaces in Java
modules.description:
Complete this assignment to demonstrate your grasp of the concepts and apply your knowledge to practical scenarios.

How to submit your assignment

  1. Click Add Submission (at the bottom)
  2. Choose your file from the computer
  3. Click Save Changes
  4. Click Edit Submission
  5. Click on the File you Uploaded
  6. Select Delete
  7. Choose your file from the computer
  8. Click Save Changes
* Upload PDF files only.

We recommend you get  Adobe Scan TM  mobile app  on your mobile devices.

Time required: x minutes

modules.name: Go Further! Additional Resources For those eager t...
modules.description:
Go Further!

Additional Resources

For those eager to expand their knowledge beyond the core content, this section offers additional resources. Here, you'll find links to further readings, advanced topics, and supplemental materials that can enrich your learning experience.

  1. Graham Lee. (2019). Modern Programming: Object Oriented Programming and Best Practices : Deconstruct Object-oriented Programming and Use It with Other Programming Paradigms to Build Applications. Packt Publishing.
  2. Mark Lassoff. (2017). Java Programming for Beginners : Java Programming for Beginners Is an Introduction to Java Programming, Taking You Through the Java Syntax and the Fundamentals of Object-oriented Programming. Packt Publishing.
  3. Gazihan Alankus, Rogério Theodoro de Brito, Basheer Ahamed Fazal, Vinicius Isola, & Miles Obare. (2019). Java Fundamentals : A Fast-paced and Pragmatic Introduction to One of the World’s Most Popular Programming Languages. Packt Publishing.
  4. Giallorenzo, S., Montesi, F., & Peressotti, M. (2024). Choral: Object-oriented Choreographic Programming. ACM Transactions on Programming Languages & Systems, 46(1), 1–59.

name: Module 8: Libraries and Packages
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about the concept of libraries and packages in software development and encapsulating related modules and classes to structure code logically and efficiently in software projects.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Introduction to libraries and packages
  2. Creating and using packages
  3. Importance of namespaces
  4. Managing dependencies with packages

modules.name: Applying Data Types in Research and Understanding Object-Oriented Programming Principles
modules.description: How can qualitative and quantitative data be utilized in research? Provide an example of how each type of data could be applied in a real-world scenario. Additionally, explain the key principles of Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) and how it enhances code reusability and maintenance compared to procedural programming.

name: Module 9: Error Handling with Exceptions
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about managing runtime errors to maintain program stability and user experience and how to handle exceptions effectively, ensuring that resources are properly managed and errors are appropriately handled in the program flow.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Explain the importance of exception handling in software development.
  2. Differentiate between checked and unchecked exceptions, understanding their characteristics.
  3. Implement Try, Catch, and Finally Blocks
  4. Create and Use Custom Exceptions and Follow Best Practices

name: Module 10: Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD)
summary:
Module Developer:  Sam NJUKI

Instructional Hours: 4 hours

{coursename}

Module Overview

In this module, you will learn about fundamental principles of OOAD, the purpose of UML in software development and class diagrams.

Learning Outcomes

By the end of this module, you should be able to:

  1. Explain the Principles of Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD).
  2. Explain the Purpose and Components of UML (Unified Modeling Language).
  3. Create and Interpret Class Diagrams.
  4. Develop and Utilize Package Diagrams.

modules.name: Advantages and Key Concepts of Object-Oriented Programming
modules.description: What are the advantages of using Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) compared to procedural programming? Discuss how OOP principles like encapsulation and inheritance contribute to these advantages. Additionally, differentiate between class and object, and explain how the concept of polymorphism enhances the flexibility of using classes and objects in programming.
